gloss:
On one fixed cyclic cover, the even-parity family of local relations is consistent, locally nonempty, and glues into a global object, while the odd-parity family (a possibilistic Popescu-Rohrlich box) is consistent, locally nonempty, yet glues nowhere. Both sides of the boundary are exhibited together on the same cover.

**Cites** `WMSpec.Transform.parity_boundary` — the corpus theorem this result restates and discharges.

**Original contribution.** The corpus proves the two-sided fact; the thesis-layer statement presents it as a single concrete exhibit demonstrating that local admissibility underdetermines the global object exactly when the cover hosts a cycle, a falsifiable example rather than an abstract dichotomy.

statement.pretty: (HiddenChannelCapacity.Consistent WMSpec.Transform.zeroBox ∧ (∀ L ∈ WMSpec.Transform.zeroBox, Finset.Nonempty (HiddenChannelCapacity.LocalStruct.rel L)) ∧ HiddenChannelCapacity.Glues WMSpec.Transform.zeroBox) ∧ HiddenChannelCapacity.Consistent HiddenChannelCapacity.prBox ∧ (∀ L ∈ HiddenChannelCapacity.prBox, Finset.Nonempty (HiddenChannelCapacity.LocalStruct.rel L)) ∧ ¬HiddenChannelCapacity.Glues HiddenChannelCapacity.prBox

title: Eidometry.Thesis.parity_boundary
